summary: This is a brief announcement post directing readers to an external article O'Neil wrote for The Guardian/Observer. The piece was published to coincide with the UK softcover launch of her book 'Weapons of Math Destruction.' The linked article addresses how algorithms can deceive and what can be done about it. The post itself contains no substantive argument, serving purely as a pointer to her journalism work elsewhere.
key_points: Tied to UK softcover book launch
External article focuses on algorithmic deception and accountability
key_insight: This post serves as a brief redirect to O'Neil's Guardian article on algorithmic accountability rather than containing original analysis.
